summary: Tested the new hidden Developer Console feature at /developer end-to-end. Backend: 13/13 pytest tests pass — dev login (success + 401 wrong creds), auth isolation (no token=401, retailer token=403, dev token cannot access /api/registrations or /api/auth/me), GET /dev/admin returns ADMIN, validation (empty user_id=400, short pw=400, collision RT10234=400), full rotation ADMIN->TESTADMIN/NewPass@123 then restored to ADMIN/Bakkar@111. Frontend: /developer renders devLoginCard with no app sidebar; after entering admin/Biki@1626 the devPanel becomes visible, devCurrentAdminId shows 'ADMIN', gps_dev_token saved in localStorage, no /dev link found anywhere in the normal app shell. Admin credentials are confirmed restored to ADMIN/Bakkar@111 (curl returns 200).

minor.issue: When body.password is omitted, only user_id is rotated; works correctly but consider stronger password policy than just length>=6 (no upper/digit/special check).
No rate-limiting / brute-force lockout on the developer console (a sensitive endpoint that can rewrite the super admin).

issues: Page background is plain dark with no branding; acceptable since it is intentionally hidden, but a tiny non-visible footer link would still be findable — confirmed no link in normal app shell.

action_items: (Optional) Add basic rate-limiting / lockout to POST /api/dev/login (sensitive — can rewrite super admin).
(Optional) Strengthen developer-set admin password policy beyond length>=6 (mix of letters/digits/specials).
critical_code_review_comments: security.py:get_current_dev correctly validates type='dev' AND role='developer' without any DB lookup — clean separation from app users. Good.
server.py:/api/dev/login uses hmac.compare_digest (constant-time) against env DEV_USERNAME/DEV_PASSWORD — good.
server.py:/api/dev/admin POST checks uniqueness against other users (excludes the admin's own id) and validates new password length — good.

Dev token issued for sub='__developer__' (not a real user id). Verified: trying to use it on get_current_user-protected endpoints fails because DB lookup returns no user — so dev tokens cannot escalate to admin endpoints. Verified end-to-end.

Frontend /developer route is handled in route() via path.startsWith('/dev') and showDevOnly() hides the regular shell; localStorage key gps_dev_token is independent of the normal gps_token — good isolation.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — dev login + panel + current-admin display + localStorage token + no leak into app nav

test_credentials: Developer: admin/Biki@1626. App Super Admin: ADMIN/Bakkar@111 (RESTORED). Retailer: RT10234/Password@123.
seed_data_creation: None permanent. Rotation test temporarily changed admin to TESTADMIN/NewPass@123 then restored to ADMIN/Bakkar@111 in finally-block (verified via curl 200 after run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Dev console JWT uses claim type='dev' role='developer' and sub='__developer__' (not a real user). The dev token cannot be used on any get_current_user-protected endpoint because that dependency does a DB lookup by sub. /developer is reached only by direct URL — there is intentionally no nav link. Note that /app/memory/test_credentials.md mentions an older 'AD-0001/Admin@123' that is now stale; current Super Admin is ADMIN/Bakkar@111 (synced from .env by seed_data on startup).
rca of the issue: N/A — new feature verified, not a bug.
